title: Occult Dread & Wrongness
description: The texture of wrong — the specific sensations, observations, and details that signal something is operating outside the expected parameters of the world.

entries.result: Everything is normal, and you are certain that it isn't.
The person you're speaking to is saying the right things and their face is doing the wrong things.
A familiar place looks exactly as it should, except one detail that you can't identify but know is wrong.
You've had this conversation before. Not recently. But before.
The room is too quiet for its location. Not muffled — absorbed.
The light in the room behaves normally everywhere except in the corner where someone is standing.
Someone near you blinks at the wrong time — too long, or in the wrong direction.
A sound that was clearly inside the building is clearly not coming from inside the building.
You can't remember entering this room. You know you did. You can't remember it.
The person you're speaking to has the hands of someone thirty years older than their face.
Everyone in the space knows each other and knows you and you don't know any of them.
You are the only one who has noticed something significant, and you cannot make anyone else look at it.
Something that should be warm is cold. Something that should be cold is warm.
A reflection in glass or water shows you doing something you are not doing.
The other person has no shadow. You check. You have two.
A room that is entirely familiar to you is laid out in a way that is subtly, persistently wrong.
Something you were holding is no longer in your hand. You did not put it down.
You have been looking at the same object for too long and it has not changed but you have.
The thing in the corner is still there. You've been ignoring it for fifteen minutes. It has been watching you ignore it.
The room feels smaller. Not claustrophobic — geometrically smaller. The walls are where they were. The room is smaller.
Everyone has stopped speaking mid-word. Not mid-sentence. Mid-word. They don't seem to notice.
The object on the table is casting a shadow. There is no light source that would cast it.
You stepped over something in the doorway. There was nothing in the doorway.
Your notes from the last hour contain information you don't remember writing down.
The building has one more floor than it has entrances to.
A person you spoke to twenty minutes ago has no memory of the conversation.
You counted the people in the room. You counted again. The number was different.
The door you came through is not on this wall anymore.
Something has been watching the crew since they arrived and they've all noticed separately and none of them have said anything.
The thing that just happened cannot have happened. It did happen. You saw it. Everyone saw it. Nobody is acknowledging it.
The air tastes like somewhere else.
A person in the room is speaking in your voice without having said anything.
The ceiling is too far away. You don't know how far away it is. It keeps being further.
You are leaving but you are not getting closer to the exit.
The thing you touched is still touching you.
Someone knows something about you that is not available to be known. They mention it conversationally.
The building is breathing. Not metaphorically.
The person at the other end of the room is very far away. They are the same distance they were a minute ago. The room hasn't changed.
You have the absolute certainty that you must not look toward the door. You cannot explain this.
Everything is fine. It was fine before. It will be fine after. The moment you stop concentrating, something will be different.
